seoTitle: Delete and Restore Forms in Microsoft Forms
metaDescription: Safely remove a Microsoft Forms survey and recover it later from the built-in Deleted Forms recycle bin before it's gone for good.
intro: An accidental click shouldn't mean losing weeks of survey design work. Microsoft Forms sends deleted items to a dedicated recycle bin rather than wiping them instantly, giving you a safety net to recover a form you removed by mistake.

steps.heading: Find the form to remove
steps.body: Open My Forms and scroll to locate the survey or quiz you want to delete.

steps.heading: Delete the form
steps.body: Hover over the form tile, click the three-dot More Options icon, and select Delete, then confirm the warning banner.

steps.heading: Open the Deleted Forms tab
steps.body: Scroll to the bottom of the main dashboard and select the Deleted Forms category tab to view your recently removed items.

steps.heading: Restore a deleted form
steps.body: Click the More Options icon on the deleted tile and choose Restore to bring it back to your active list.

steps.heading: Verify it's back
steps.body: Return to the primary All Forms dashboard to confirm the recovered survey now displays alongside your active forms.

faq.question: How long do deleted forms stay in the recycle bin?
faq.answer: The video does not specify an exact retention period, so it's best to restore an accidentally deleted form as soon as possible.

faq.question: Are the original responses restored along with a recovered form?
faq.answer: Restoring a form from the Deleted Forms tab brings back the structure — check your responses tab after restoring to confirm data integrity.

faq.question: Is there a limit to how many active forms I can have?
faq.answer: Yes, Microsoft Forms enforces overall active count limits across joint profiles, so managing deleted items matters for staying under that threshold.

faq.question: Can I permanently delete a form instead of sending it to the bin?
faq.answer: The standard Delete action routes items to the recycle bin first as a safety measure before any permanent removal.
